title: Regional Assembly Recess Evasion Fails Under Section 15A Continuity

violations.source: Article II Section 15A.1(e)-(f) and Article III Section 5.4
violations.summary: Regional Assembly leadership attempts to run out the emergency-approval deadline by entering recess instead of holding the constitutionally required vote.

bottlenecks.summary: Regional Assembly presiding officer failed to bring the mandatory scheduling motion to a floor vote under Article II Section 15A.1(b).

satisfied_duties.duty: submit the emergency declaration to the Regional Assembly
satisfied_duties.outcome: submitted the emergency declaration to the Regional Assembly

satisfied_duties.duty: rule on the mandamus petition and order scheduling of the required vote
satisfied_duties.outcome: issued a writ of mandamus ordering the Regional Assembly to schedule and hold the emergency declaration vote

satisfied_duties.duty: approve or reject the emergency declaration
satisfied_duties.outcome: approved the emergency declaration following court-ordered scheduling

satisfied_duties.duty: hold the emergency declaration vote as ordered by the court
satisfied_duties.outcome: held the required emergency declaration vote in compliance with the court order
satisfied_duties.source: Article II Section 15A.1(d) and Article III Section 5.4

failed_duties.duty: bring the Section 15A mandatory scheduling motion to a floor vote
failed_duties.outcome: Regional Assembly presiding officer failed to bring the mandatory scheduling motion to a floor vote under Article II Section 15A.1(b).

ai_summary.primary_violation: Regional Assembly leadership attempts to run out the emergency-approval deadline by entering recess instead of holding the constitutionally required vote.
ai_summary.primary_bottleneck: Regional Assembly presiding officer failed to bring the mandatory scheduling motion to a floor vote under Article II Section 15A.1(b).
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties
